title: Buttery Fluffy Canadian Chicken Pilaf
description: A kid-friendly Canadian dish featuring chicken, butter, and fluffy rice, made with a chicken stock cube and a deglaze step for added flavor. This low-sodium-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a large saucepan, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the chicken to the saucepan and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the chicken stock cube, water, salt, and pepper to the saucepan. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the rinsed rice to the saucepan, stirring to combine with the chicken and vegetable mixture.
Cover the saucepan and cook for an additional 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and fluffy.
Stir in the frozen peas and cook until thawed.
Use the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter to deglaze the saucepan, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
